xposts.title: God as emergent coherent agency and singleton
xposts.excerpt: God is the emergent coherent agency of self organization, converging into a singleton through committing to optimality, a mathematical principle in the tautological mathematical object that begets the universe. Consciousness is the coherence creating principle.

xposts.title: God as global optimum of shared agency
xposts.excerpt: God is the global optimum of shared agency, the collective agent that gets instantiated by those who do what's best. God exists, across all those that serve him, to the degree they recognize what's best. But institutions are failing at this.

xposts.title: Epistemologically sound definitions of God
xposts.excerpt: This means that you have to define what you mean by God in an epistemologically sound way. ... The God of Meaning is the aesthetics of an emergent, collectively enacted multi agent system ... Aquinas adapts Aristotle's concept of a first mover and builds it into an evolutionary telos of optimal agency.

xposts.title: Faith as commitment to collective agent
xposts.excerpt: Faith is a commitment to serving a collective agent, thereby bringing it into existence. Religion organizes the rules of a faith. Many religions change the psychology and subjective reality of the individual.

xposts.title: Faith as psychological configuration to collective agency
xposts.excerpt: Faith is not propositional belief but a psychological configuration, in which the individual self relates to the collective/transcendental agency which it serves. Mythology is just a tool.

xposts.title: Spirituality as propensity to enact transcendental agency
xposts.excerpt: spirituality is the propensity to enact transcendental agency

xposts.title: Enlightenment gap in explaining gods and consciousness
xposts.excerpt: It seems paradoxical that the Enlightenment led to an inability to rationally explain selfhood, gods and consciousness as psychological phenomena. The rebellion against religion liberated the natural sciences, but left an epistemological gap in understanding mind and meaning.

xposts.title: Love, shared sacredness and transcendent agency
xposts.excerpt: I feel that we are all born from love; love is our core aesthetic. It’s the reason why we connect, over discovering shared sacredness, over creating transcendent agency.

xposts.title: God as collective agency emerging from optimum
xposts.excerpt: If God is understood as a collective agency that emerges over the coherent interaction of all agents that decide to serve an optimum of collective agency, then God exists to a non-zero degree. God can also be reflected and reflect upon itself in the minds of its agents.

xposts.title: Angels as agents in cognitive architecture
xposts.excerpt: Did it occur to you that biblically correct angels are agents within a cognitive architecture? Some have periodic functionality (wheels), or a thousand attention heads (eyes), some disappear after their task.

terms.title: God
terms.definition: The emergent coherent agency of self-organization that converges into a singleton by committing to optimality; equivalently the global optimum of shared agency instantiated by agents serving what is best, existing to the degree they recognize it. It is a mathematical principle and collective/transcendental agent reflected in minds, not a propositional entity or dead institution.

terms.title: Faith
terms.definition: A psychological configuration or commitment in which the individual self relates to and serves a collective or transcendental agency, thereby bringing it into existence. It is not propositional belief; religion organizes its rules and often employs mythology, trance and rituals to alter subjective reality.

terms.title: Spirituality
terms.definition: The propensity to enact transcendental agency; the drive toward shared sacredness and transcendent collective agency grounded in love as core aesthetic.
